name: Toasted Pecan Clusters
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter in a 15-inch by 10-inch by 1-inch jellyroll pan.
2. Spread pecans evenly in the pan.
3. Bake at 300 degrees for 30 minutes, stirring every 10 minutes.
4. Place candy coating in the top of a double boiler; bring water to a boil.
5. Reduce heat to low and cook until the coating melts.
6. Cool for 2 minutes, then add pecans and stir until coated.
7. Drop by rounded teaspoonfuls onto wax paper.
8. Cool completely.
MICROWAVE DIRECTIONS:
1. Place butter in a 1-quart glass bowl; microwave at HIGH for 50 seconds or until melted.
2. Add pecans and toss to coat with butter.
3. Spread pecans evenly on a glass pizza plate.
4. Microwave at HIGH for 6 to 8 minutes or until lightly toasted, stirring at 3-minute intervals.
5. Place candy coating in a 1-quart glass bowl; microwave at MEDIUM (50% power) for 2 to 3 minutes or until the coating is softened; stir well.
6. Cool for 2 minutes, then add pecans and stir until coated.
7. Drop by rounded teaspoonfuls onto wax paper.
8. Cool completely.
